content: I noticed that things like list_head and mutex are present in the uapi crate. Why is that happening? It doesn't sound right to me.
So we still use -I./include for uapi_generated.rs, and that could include include/linux/ethtool.h instead of include/uapi/linux/ethtool.h I think.

How in the world does this work normally? So #include <linux/ethtool.h> should normally include the uapi version when used from a uapi/ header, but when used in the kernel it should include the non-uapi version ... or what?

Here is my understanding:

  • when kernel includes linux/ethtool.h, it'll use the non-uapi version, because the -I./include is before the -I./include/uapi.

  • when a userspace includes linux/ethtool.h, it normally cannot access non-uapi headers (because they are not installed in /usr/include directory), so it will use the uapi version.
well, kernel headers can be installed, but it's pointless for userspace to use them, so I think they will not be added as the include path of a userspace program.
